channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Paris Hilton-backed youth welfare bill passes House, heads to Biden's desk
item.description: Today is a day I will never forget. After years of sharing my story and advocating on Capitol Hill, the Stop Institutional Child Abuse Act has officially passed the U.S. Congress,” Hilton said in a post on X.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Government funding to run out unless Speaker Mike Johnson finds support for bill
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Rushing to prevent a Christmastime government shutdown, House Speaker Mike Johnson scrambled Wednesday to salvage a stopgap measure to keep federal offices running despite an onslaught of opposition from hard-right conservatives led by President-elect Donald Trump -ally Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Two bald eagles struck by vehicles in Michigan in as many days: DNR
item.description: Unfortunately, the DNR says eagle-vehicle collisions are somewhat common, especially along highways with faster speed limits.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, NewsNation

item.title: Charlotte Hornets apologize after PS5 taken from 13-year-old during on-court giveaway
item.description: What was supposed to be a festive "skit" during the Charlotte Hornets game against the Philadelphia 76ers on Monday ended up earning the Hornets ‘Grinch’-like jeers on social media.
item.dc:creator: Ciara Lankford, WFLA, NBC

item.title: House passes bill backed by Paris Hilton to reform youth treatment facilities
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The House passed legislation Wednesday requiring more oversight of youth residential treatment facilities, a feat for hotel heiress Paris Hilton who has spent years lobbying lawmakers to regulate an industry marred by child abuse allegations.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: NCAA president says there are 'less than 10' transgender athletes in college sports
item.description: NCAA President Charlie Baker told a Senate panel that there are fewer than 10 transgender athletes he is aware of who currently compete in college sports, pouring cold water on an issue Republicans have said is a nationwide problem and one that is increasingly fraught territory for Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, Fox 5

item.title: US effort to curb China's and Russia's access to advanced computer chips 'inadequate,' report finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Commerce Department's efforts to curb China's and Russia's access to American-made advanced computer chips have been “inadequate” and will need more funding to stymie their ability to manufacture advanced weapons, according to a report published Wednesday by the Senate's Perman
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Oldest known stone tablet inscribed with the Ten Commandments sells for over $5M
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The oldest known stone tablet inscribed with the Ten Commandments sold for more than $5 million at an auction on Wednesday. Sotheby’s said the 155-pound (52-kilogram) marble slab was acquired by an anonymous buyer who plans to donate it to an Israeli institution.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former New Orleans priest, 93, is sentenced to life in prison for raping boy decades ago
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — A judge sentenced a 93-year-old former Catholic priest Wednesday to spend the rest of his life behind bars for raping a teenage boy decades ago.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Royal Caribbean passenger dies soon after being detained for chaotic incident caught on video
item.description: A Royal Caribbean cruise ship passenger was recently caught on camera acting unruly before being taken down by security. He died an hour later.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What is 'coffee badging?' New workplace trend seen as protest to return-to-office push
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- First, it was Amazon, dropping the boom on remote work and ordering workers back to the office five days a week, beginning next month.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: District attorney indicted after shooting at fugitive suspect, striking home with 3 kids inside: police
item.description: A Tennessee grand jury has indicted District Attorney Christopher Stanford on one count of reckless endangerment after he allegedly fired shots at a fugitive, striking a house.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Scientists find 'forever chemicals' lurking in certain smartwatch wristbands
item.description: Prior research has shown that more than a fifth of Americans wear a smartwatch or a fitness tracker — and they do so for extended periods of time.
item.dc:creator: Sharon Udasin, ABC 4

item.title: Hochul vows to haul Luigi Mangione back to New York to face terror charges in UnitedHealthcare CEO's slaying
item.description: New York Gov. Kathy Hochul demanded UnitedHealthcare CEO suspected killer Luigi Mangione be brought back from Pennsylvania to face new terrorism charges.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Here's what made the cut in Congress’ 1,500-page government funding deal
item.description: Congress on Tuesday rolled out its last major funding deal of the year and is expected to move quickly to try to pass it ahead of a Friday government shutdown deadline.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Democracies across the globe are at a crossroads, as authoritarians seek to chip away at freedoms
item.description: In November, the world's most powerful democracy elected as its next president a man who schemed to overturn its last presidential election. A month later, South Koreans swarmed their legislature to block their president's attempt to impose martial law.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 found dead in West Valley City homicide, investigation ongoing
item.description: Five have been confirmed dead and another injured in an apparent homicide at a home in West Valley City.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Drachman, NewsNation

item.title: FBI's warning as reported drone sightings plague the East Coast and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: GOP report recommends Liz Cheney be criminally investigated over Jan. 6 work
item.description: It’s a claim that if pursued would likely face significant roadblocks but that nonetheless comes as Trump has suggested members of the Jan. 6 panel should “go to jail” for their work.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KRON4

item.title: Republicans butt heads on daylight saving time after Trump directive
item.description: A Republican brouhaha is emerging after President-elect Trump called to eliminate daylight saving time, setting up battle lines between prominent GOP lawmakers in their hopes to lock the clock. Trump waded into the longtime sore subject Friday, surprising — and perplexing — some lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Indiana law shrouds executions in secrecy, prompting new pushes for public oversight
item.description: From public hangings in the town square to lethal injections witnessed by journalists, executions historically have mostly been carried out with at least some public scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Amazon has up to 20% off gift cards ahead of the holidays
item.description: You can save on a wide range of gift cards, making it easier and more affordable to give the gift of shopping and saving.
item.dc:creator: Christina Marfice, KTLA 5

item.title: Key details about the man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare's CEO
item.description: Luigi Mangione, the man accused of killing the CEO of UnitedHealthcare, was charged in New York with murder as an act of terrorism, prosecutors announced Tuesday. Mangione, 26, is being held without bail in Pennsylvania, where he was arrested at a McDonald’s on Dec.
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE and SARAH BRUMFIELD, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Stock market today: Wall Street trims its stellar gains, Dow slides again
item.description: The Fed is widely expected to announce the third cut of the year to its main interest rate on Wednesday, and officials are also scheduled to unveil projections about where they see rates heading in upcoming years.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Shock poll: 41 percent of young voters find killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO acceptable
item.description: Anger over health insurance companies has been in the spotlight after Thompson was fatally shot Dec. 4 in New York City.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: What we know about the 2 killed, 6 injured in Wisconsin Christian school shooting
item.description: A student and teacher were killed and six others — five students and a teacher — were injured in Monday's shooting.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: Oscar Mayer accepting applications for the newest class of Wienermobile drivers
item.description: Oscar Mayer is now accepting applications for its coveted “Hotdogger” position, offering recent college graduates the chance to drive the brand’s iconic 27-foot Wienermobile across the country.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: FTC bans junk ticket, hotel fees
item.description: The final rule will become effective 120 days after its publication in the Federal Register, coinciding with the first few months of Trump’s administration.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: A timeline of the investigation of the Gilgo Beach killings
item.description: For years, women had been disappearing on Long Island. Then in 2010, investigators searching for a missing woman began finding 10 sets of human remains in the scrub along a barrier island parkway, not far from the sands of New York's remote Gilgo Beach.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Suspect in Gilgo Beach serial killings is charged in the death of a seventh woman
item.description: The New York architect facing murder charges in a string of deaths known as the Gilgo Beach killings was charged on Tuesday in the death of a seventh woman.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press via Nexstar Media Wire, KRON4

item.title: Judge sentences Zephen Xaver, man who executed 5 women at Florida bank, to death
item.description: Zephen Xaver pleaded guilty to five counts of murder for killing Ana Piñon Williams, Debra Cook, Marisol Lopez, Cynthia Watson and Jessica Montague at a SunTrust Bank in Sebring, Florida in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Marino, KRON4

item.title: Trump, Softbank CEO announce $100B SoftBank investment in the US
item.description: resident-elect Donald Trump will join SoftBank Group CEO Masayoshi Son at his Florida home on Monday to announce that the Japanese company is planning to invest $100 billion in U.S. projects over the next four years.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Long, Fox 5

item.title: House bill would ban 'unnecessary' genital surgeries for intersex foster youth
item.description: States receiving federal funding for foster care and adoption assistance programs would be required to prohibit certain surgeries for foster children born with intersex traits under legislation introduced Monday by Rep. Sylvia Garcia (D-Texas).
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, WFLA, NBC

item.title: McConnell urges Trump to reject isolationism
item.description: Outgoing Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (R-Ky.) is calling on President-elect Trump to reject isolationists in the Republican Party who will argue for the United States to focus on China at the expense of Ukraine and other areas of the world.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Point guard Dennis Schroder traded by Brooklyn to Golden State
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — By adding Dennis Schroder, the Golden State Warriors believe they have found the perfect backcourt mate to complement Stephen Curry. Curry can't wait to get going in what he expects to be a “seamless transition for him.” “It can help me, it can help us,” Curry said.
item.dc:creator: JANIE McCAULEY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Former longtime South Carolina congressman John Spratt dies at 82
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — John Spratt, a former longtime Democratic congressman from South Carolina who successfully pushed for a balanced budget deal in the 1990s but was unseated decades later when his district turned Republican, has died. He was 82.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Private Sin: How a church protected child molesters
item.description: Protestant Reformed Churches in America is struggling with what it has called the "private sin" of child sexual abuse and decades of covering up for abusers, including church leaders and school teachers.
item.dc:creator: Ken Kolker, ABC 4
